LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT FLEMING'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS OFF CALENDAR. THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B AND PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF HER MOTION TO SET ASIDE. THE MOVING PARTY DID NOT E-MAIL A COPY OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG AS SOON AS POSSIBLE OR PROVIDE A COPY OF THEM AT TODAY'S HEARING AS DIRECTED IN THE TENTATIVE RULING. Motion For Reconsideration Of Court'S Interim Order Issued June 24, 2013
Off Calendar Nov-22-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ELAINE FLEMING, 415.269.6188, DEFENDANT IN PRO PER; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Motion For Reconsideration Of Court's Interim Order Issued June 24, 2013 IS Off calendar. Defendant did not provide courtesy copies of "all papers filed in support of'[the] motion to set aside default that was originally noticed for hearing on June 24, 2013" in violation of the October 18, 2013 Order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ELAINE FLEMING'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Appearances: Elaine Fleming (415.269.6188) defendant and responding party appearing in propria persona; No other appearances.

Notice was not given to plaintiff of defendant's intent to appear this morning. Discussed and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment is off calendar. On April 10, 2013, the clerk entered default against the moving party, Defendant Elaine H. Fleming, an individual. "Entry of defendant's default instantaneously cuts off its right to appear in the action. The defendant is 'out of court.' [She] has no right to participate in the proceedings until either (a) its default is set aside (in which event, it may respond to the complaint), or (b) a default judgment is entered (in which event, [she] may appeal)." (Weil & Brown, Civil Procedure Before Trial 5:6.) Accordingly, Ms. Fleming's filing of the motion for summary judgment is improper. Further, the Court, sua sponte, intends to reconsider its interim order issued on June 24, 2013 taking Ms. Fleming's motion to set aside default "off calendar as Plaintiff's request for default was rejected on May 22, 2013," and to consider the merits of Ms. Fleming's motion to set aside default. The interim order appears to have been in error. It appears that default had already been entered against Ms. Fleming on April 10, 2013. The Court will hold a hearing on November 22, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 302 to reconsider its interim order of June 24, 2013. If they wish, the parties may file and serve briefs regarding the Court's sua sponte motion to reconsider its interim order of June 24, 2013. Ms. Fleming may file and serve a brief, no longer than 10 pages, no later than November 8, 2013 at 4 pm. Cach, LLC may file and serve a responsive brief, no longer than 10 pages, no later than November 15, 2013 at 4 pm. Courtesy copies of these papers, including proofs of service, shall be delivered and lodged with Department 302 on the date of filing.
The parties shall also provide courtesy copies of all papers filed in support of and in opposition to Ms. Fleming's motion to set aside default that was originally noticed for hearing on June 24, 2013. Courtesy copies shall be delivered to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date of November 22, 2013. These papers shall be provided by November 8, 2013 at 4 pm. The Court has served a copy of this order on the parties on October 18, 2013. A copy of this order was provided to Ms. Fleming in Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR #6185 (302/MJM)
